profiles.summary: Exact CCR-CLASSC-C-001 data-client profile: WAE, WML, WBXML, WMLScript, WMLScript libraries, caching, WSP, WDP, and WCMP mandatory client features. WTP becomes mandatory only when connection-mode WSP is supported; security, push, telephony, identity, and UAProf remain separately declared.
Adds mandatory telephony, push, and security profile behavior after strict Class C closure.
Makes mandatory and optional release features mandatory; not part of the first compliance release.
Modern features and improved behavior that preserve strict results or are explicitly separated from strict mode.

sprints.title: Authoritative source control plane
sprints.goal: Lock the complete release, its supporting assets, class governance, and legal retrieval posture before implementation claims are expanded.

acceptance: All 97 technical release members have authority URLs, hashes, sizes, family classifications, and local-state comparisons.
Every release member belongs to one family and approved base/SIN order is explicit; proposed documents are excluded from normative sequences.
Channel, WML, PAP, SI, SL, and WTA-WML DTD URLs, sizes, and SHA-256 hashes are recorded without committing restricted content.

outputs: Verified WAP-215 Class Conformance Requirements source lock
acceptance: The official WAP Forum WAP-215 payload is byte-locked and all six Class A/B/C client/server tables are extracted with exact identifiers and resolved release families.
Every normative external reference in strict-target families has authority metadata and is classified as definition-only, implementation-relevant, or test-relevant.
The selected TIAEIA-732 bearer citation has normalized part/version metadata and an explicit licensed-access disposition.

outputs: Approved staged recovery of 27 missing and 3 divergent high-value release members
Verified cleaned-source promotion and append-only provenance snapshot
acceptance: All 97 technical members and text extractions have private hash/size evidence before promotion.
Redistribution is explicitly approved before binaries or parsed derivatives enter Git; afterward each promoted source matches the source lock and passes the existing manual cleanup workflow.

exitGates: Release and effective-spec validators pass.
The WAP-215 source lock and exact selected Class C client graph pass validation.
No recovered binary or parsed derivative is committed without redistribution approval.

sprints.title: CCR, SCR, and normative obligation ledgers
sprints.goal: Replace thematic coverage estimates with one-to-one conformance accounting for the selected client profile.

outputs: Class C client CCR dependency graph
acceptance: Every Class A/B/C client and server dependency is extracted from WAP-215, and every selected Class C client dependency resolves to an effective release family and feature group.

outputs: Machine-readable effective SCR ledger for every Class C family
acceptance: Every effective SCR line has source document, section, actor, M/O status, dependency expression, strict disposition, and review state.

outputs: Normative clause ledger nested under SCR features
acceptance: All 201 selected rows across the nine mandatory Class C families have nested normative clauses anchored and deduplicated.
Normative text is not reduced to keyword search: implicit MUST rules and clauses within each SCR feature are anchored and deduplicated.
The completed ledger declares all nine covered families, zero remaining selected rows, and a closed 201-row gate.

outputs: Strict, conditional, deferred, and Waves-extension disposition registry
Nine family SCR ledgers with strict and Class C dispositions
acceptance: No optional feature is silently treated as mandatory or vice versa; extensions cannot satisfy strict obligations.
Every mandatory Class C SCR line maps to implemented evidence or one additive open work item; thematic RQ groups may map many-to-many but cannot hide an unmapped SCR.
Requirement index, traceability, test coverage, and work program cannot disagree on covered/planned/deferred status without an explicit exception.
Every selected-row behavior implemented from successor-era material is classified as compatible, strict correction required, or successor-only.
Family-specific WAE, WDP/WCMP, and WSP delta registers feed one machine-checkable cross-family view; WML, WBXML, WMLScript, libraries, and caching are not omitted.

exitGates: Every mandatory selected-profile SCR has an owner and evidence state.
Every optional SCR has a declared capability or deferral.
No compliance percentage is calculated from thematic RQ groups alone.

sprints.title: WML parser, deck model, and validation baseline
sprints.goal: Make effective WML 1.3 structure and validation deterministic before closing higher-order runtime behavior.

acceptance: The exact WML-C-01..59, WML-S-60..69, and WML-C-70..76 sequence retains source identity, actor, M/O status, profile applicability, and evidence mapping.
Grammar, uniqueness, ordering, template inheritance, and metadata retention match effective WML 1.3 with deterministic errors.

outputs: Direct conformance evidence for the three WBXML:MCF client rows
acceptance: Text WML and WBXML-derived WML reach equivalent deck models; strict DTD identity and unknown-DTD behavior are explicit.
WBXML-C-001, WBXML-C-010, and WBXML-C-011 are fixture-backed against a pinned decoder; fake fixed-output and either-result fixtures do not satisfy this gate.

outputs: Complete field/control syntax and attribute validation
acceptance: Input/select/option/optgroup/fieldset constraints, masks, defaults, and validation failures are deterministic.
Malformed XML, invalid WML, unsupported optional constructs, and recoverable content errors have spec-shaped deterministic outcomes.

exitGates: All strict WML structures parse or fail deterministically.
Text and WBXML input parity is fixture-backed.
The 76-item SCR matrix has no unmapped mandatory parser obligation.

sprints.title: WML state, tasks, events, forms, and navigation
sprints.goal: Close the observable runtime mechanics that define historical WML browser behavior.

outputs: Deck/card context, history, newcontext, and inter-card process-order closure
acceptance: Forward, backward, reload, failure rollback, and context reset match strict ordering across native and WASM adapters.
Initialization, escaping modes, substitution contexts, setvar ordering, and invalid references match effective WML rules.

outputs: do/onevent/task shadowing and softkey precedence closure
acceptance: go, prev, refresh, noop, intrinsic events, template/card shadowing, BACK availability, and activation order are deterministic.
Field commit, postfield generation, same-deck suppression, accept-charset, URL encoding, multipart optionality, referer, and no-cache behavior follow SIN 105.
Timer start, stop, resume, expiry, invalid values, ontimer dispatch, and host wakeups are deterministic and target-parity tested.

outputs: Access control, low-memory, and runtime error policy
acceptance: Deck access checks, low-memory behavior, unknown DTDs, task failures, and user-visible errors have strict deterministic policies.

exitGates: Mandatory WML runtime SCR lines are covered by deterministic tests.
Form/network effects cross contracts without host-side semantic reimplementation.
Native and WASM outcomes are equivalent.

sprints.title: Historical rendering, focus, and input fidelity
sprints.goal: Complete the visible and interactive WML 1.3 behavior expected from a data-only client.

outputs: Inline flow, wrapping, paragraph alignment, and wrap-mode fidelity
acceptance: Layout is deterministic under constrained viewports and preserves focus/navigation order.
Columns, cells, whitespace preservation, line breaks, and constrained overflow follow WML semantics.
Mandatory alt behavior and supported image rendering are correct; optional spacing/alignment/size hints are explicitly claimed or deferred.

outputs: Input/select focus, masks, tabindex, and commit behavior
acceptance: Historical keypad/softkey traversal is deterministic; optional tabindex is capability-declared and tested when enabled.
Representative WML decks replay to stable render lists, focus states, history, and emitted effects across native and WASM.

exitGates: Mandatory visual/interactive SCR behavior has golden evidence.
Optional render hints are capability-declared.
No rendering semantics are implemented in transport or host adapters.

sprints.title: WMLScript language, bytecode, VM, and libraries
sprints.goal: Close the mandatory Class C script engine and standard-library behavior with bounded deterministic execution.

outputs: Complete bytecode decoder and structural verifier
acceptance: The 41 mandatory WMLScript:MCF rows remain exactly mapped while WAP header, constant/pragma/function pools, instruction families, indexes, jumps, stack/local bounds, and malformed input failure are fully verified before execution.
Types, conversions, operators, calls, locals, return values, control flow, and invalid-operation semantics match the effective specification.

outputs: Extern, pragma, URL invocation, and access-control closure
acceptance: Script loading and invocation respect content identity, access domains/paths, pragmas, argument binding, and deterministic failure.

outputs: Complete standard-library conformance matrix and implementation
acceptance: All 80 mandatory WMLScriptLibs:MCF rows map to exact source IDs, implementation status, open work, and deterministic tests; Lang, Float, String, URL, WMLBrowser, and Dialogs close their nested clauses; optional immediate refresh is declared.

outputs: Host capability and script error boundary closure
acceptance: Dialogs, timers, navigation effects, fatal/non-fatal errors, resource limits, and post-invocation effect ordering are deterministic.
MIME handling, compiled-script decode, cache identity, fetch ownership, and engine invocation preserve layer boundaries.

exitGates: Mandatory WMLScript and library SCR lines have executable evidence.
Malformed bytecode cannot reach execution.
Native/WASM and host capability outcomes are equivalent.

sprints.title: WAE, caching, formats, and content behavior
sprints.goal: Close browser-level WAE behavior that composes markup, script, media, cache, and request policy.

outputs: Effective WAP 1.2.1 WAE + SIN behavior map
acceptance: WAE requirements come from WAP-190 plus SINs, with WAP-236 used only for explicit successor deltas.

outputs: Media type, charset, language, URI, and capability behavior closure
acceptance: Accepted content, character decoding, language/capability headers, URI limits, and unsupported content outcomes match the selected profile.

outputs: User-agent cache model and invalidation closure
acceptance: All five mandatory WAPCachingMod:MCF rows map to exact source IDs, implementation status, open work, and direct tests; zero-byte mode is explicit; both must-revalidate history branches, intra-resource behavior, and cache security are deterministic.
Mandatory WBMP decode/display and fallback behavior are capability-tested without relying on modern browser image semantics.

outputs: Unified user-agent error and recovery policy
acceptance: Content, script, navigation, access, memory, and unsupported-feature errors have strict observable behavior and diagnostic-only enhancements.
Each current WAP 2.0-derived behavior is confirmed compatible, changed for strict mode, or isolated behind a successor/extension profile.

outputs: WAP 1.2.1 HTTP Basic authentication challenge and credential-flow closure
acceptance: The browser and transport implement deterministic Basic challenge parsing, credential acquisition/storage policy, Authorization retry behavior, cancellation, and user-visible failure without treating header token registration as conformance.

exitGates: Class C browser/WAE mandatory groups resolve to tested behavior.
Cache and media behavior are independent of modern DOM assumptions.
WAP 2.0 successor behavior cannot silently leak into strict mode.

sprints.title: WDP, WCMP, and conditional WTP Class C transport core
sprints.goal: Complete the historical datagram and transaction mechanics required beneath WSP.

outputs: Effective WDP service, addressing, port, and bearer profile
acceptance: All nine selected CDPD/IPv4 WDP path rows map to implementation evidence or corrective work; the normalized informative TIAEIA-732 dependency retains its licensed-payload metadata-only boundary.
Payload limits, truncation/rejection, segmentation applicability, and reassembly failure are deterministic for the selected bearer.
The five selected general-WCMP dependency rows are implemented and tested; all 62 source rows retain exact capability disposition using the WAP 1.2.1 byte identity.

outputs: Effective WTP PDU and transaction state machines
acceptance: When connection-oriented WSP is claimed, classes, invoke/result/ack/abort, TID, duplicate handling, retransmission, and terminal states map to effective WTP SCR entries.

outputs: WTP timing, NACK, hold-off, and retry closure
acceptance: When connection-oriented WSP is claimed, all WTP timers and retry limits use deterministic simulated time and incorporate SIN corrections in order.

outputs: WDP/WTP packet and replay golden corpus
acceptance: Positive, boundary, malformed, duplicate, timeout, and abort traces replay identically and cite exact effective source features.
Current successor-spec implementation assumptions are either proven compatible or ticketed as strict-mode corrections.

exitGates: The nine-row selected WDP path and five-row selected WCMP path have executable evidence.
WTP evidence is required only when connection-oriented WSP is claimed.
Timing tests are deterministic and do not require live networks.
WAP 2.0 transport deltas are explicit.

sprints.title: WSP session, connectionless, headers, and host fetch
sprints.goal: Complete the WAP 1.2.1 session layer and make it the real desktop ingress path.

outputs: Effective WSP PDU, primitive, and method matrix
acceptance: All 109 WSP rows retain exact source disposition and the eight-row selected connectionless path maps to implementation evidence or corrective work.
Known tokens, code pages, text fallbacks, unknown policy, encoding versions, and round trips match the WAP 1.2.1 effective set.

outputs: WSP capability negotiation and session lifecycle
acceptance: Connect, capability bounds, suspend/resume, disconnect, re-establishment, and overage behavior are deterministic.

outputs: Native WAP transport desktop GET ingress
acceptance: Browser mode selection, native fetch, fallback policy, error mapping, and Kannel smoke behavior preserve the generated transport contract.
Effective WML form data crosses engine, host, connectionless WSP, and WDP with exact method/content/request-policy semantics; WTP is activated only for connection-oriented WSP.
WAP-230-derived behavior is classified as compatible, strict correction, or successor-only.

exitGates: The eight-row selected connectionless WSP path has executable evidence.
Connection-oriented WSP and WTP remain separately capability-gated.
Desktop GET and POST use the native stack with explicit fallback.
Generated Rust/TypeScript transport contracts have no drift.

sprints.title: Cross-layer parity and interoperability closure
sprints.goal: Prove the complete selected-profile behavior across source, engine, transport, gateway compatibility, and desktop host.

outputs: End-to-end conformance fixture ledger and deterministic replay runner
acceptance: Every mandatory selected-profile feature participates in at least one unit, boundary, integration, or end-to-end evidence path.
Deck load, navigation, focus, render output, scripts, timers, effects, and traces are byte/semantic equivalent across targets.
Selected WAP transactions interoperate with Kannel and replay deterministically without allowing implementation behavior to override normative sources.
A real WAP deck can traverse the entire strict stack with deterministic session history, form submission, script effects, and rendered output.

outputs: Malformed, constrained-resource, and recovery stress suite
acceptance: Malformed source, constrained payloads, timeouts, duplicate packets, memory pressure, and script limits never crash or produce nondeterministic state.

outputs: Evidence bundle with source, build, test, fixture, and known-deviation identities
acceptance: A release candidate can be independently audited without inferring coverage from ticket status or source-file count.

exitGates: Cross-layer strict scenarios pass with native/WASM parity.
Interop evidence supplements rather than replaces normative evidence.
Every known deviation is explicit and ticketed.

sprints.title: Strict Class C-compatible release evidence gate
sprints.goal: Reach an evidence-backed strict compatibility release without making an unsupported historical certification claim.

acceptance: All mandatory selected-profile features are claimed with evidence; optional features are explicitly supported or not supported; conditional WTP applicability is disclosed.
No mandatory selected-profile CCR/SCR/normalized clause remains missing, ambiguous, or evidenced only by prose.
Engine, transport, host, frontend tests, contracts, typecheck, lint, WASM build, and desktop production build pass from documented toolchains.
The malformed WebKit scrollbar selector no longer blocks CSS minification and a clean worktree production build succeeds under the declared Node/pnpm runtime.
Documentation distinguishes protocol/spec compatibility from WAP Forum certification and trademark rights.
All strict golden outputs are versioned and become the differential floor for later enhancements.

exitGates: No mandatory selected-profile obligation is unmapped.
All clean builds and tests pass.
Compatibility claims identify CCR-CLASSC-C-001, disclose optional and conditional profiles, and do not imply formal certification.

sprints.title: Conditional security and optional WAP profiles
sprints.goal: Add historically compliant optional capability profiles without expanding the strict Class C floor.

outputs: WTLS conditional profile and effective SCR ledger
acceptance: Record, alert, handshake, crypto, key exchange, certificate, and failure behavior are either implemented/tested or explicitly not claimed.
Identity-module capabilities remain separate from the data-only strict profile and have explicit host/security boundaries.
Push activation, content types, DTDs, PPG/PAP boundaries, SI/SL behavior, and cache operation are fully profile-gated.
evidence: manual: optional push profile conformance suite

acceptance: Telephony functions and bearer addenda remain capability- and platform-gated and cannot affect Class C strict behavior.
evidence: manual: optional WTA profile conformance suite

acceptance: Capability description, advertisement, and privacy behavior are explicit and do not change unprofiled strict requests.
Approved optional features receive isolated profiles; proposed-only documents remain historical and cannot create strict requirements.

exitGates: Each optional profile has its own capability declaration and test lane.
Strict Class C outcomes are unchanged when optional profiles are disabled.
Proposed documents remain non-normative.

sprints.title: Behavior-preserving enhancements and Waves extensions
sprints.goal: Improve the historical stack without weakening or obscuring strict compatibility.

outputs: Strict/enhanced capability registry and mode contract
acceptance: Every extension declares whether it is invisible to strict results or requires enhanced mode.
Behavior-preserving optimizations produce identical strict traces, render lists, network PDUs, and errors.
Caching, parsing, layout, transport, and startup optimizations improve measured performance without changing strict observable outcomes.
evidence: manual: benchmark plus strict differential report

acceptance: Diagnostics expose strict state without becoming a semantic dependency or changing timing/order.

outputs: Modern accessibility, onboarding, and usability layer
acceptance: Modern UX can wrap the emulator while keypad, softkey, focus, deck, and network mechanics remain strict inside the emulation boundary.
evidence: manual: accessibility and strict differential review

exitGates: Every extension is registered and mode-scoped.
Strict differential tests pass.
Enhancements cannot satisfy or waive a strict conformance obligation.
